   289→let extract_vector_from_segment ~(verts : vec3 array) ~start_side ~end_side =
   290→  let sv_start = side_to_verts.(start_side) in
   291→  let sv_end = side_to_verts.(end_side) in
   292→  let vs = ref (0, 0, 0) in
   293→  let ve = ref (0, 0, 0) in
   294→  for i = 0 to 3 do
   295→    vs := Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:!vs ~b:verts.(sv_start.(i));
   296→    ve := Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:!ve ~b:verts.(sv_end.(i))
   297→  done;
   298→  let diff = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:!ve ~b:!vs in
   299→  Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:diff ~k:(0x10000 / 4)

   302→let extract_orient_from_segment (verts : vec3 array) =
   303→  let fvec = extract_vector_from_segment ~verts ~start_side:wfront ~end_side:wback in
   304→  let uvec = extract_vector_from_segment ~verts ~start_side:wbottom ~end_side:wtop in
   305→  Ox_math.vm_vector_2_matrix ~fvec ~uvec:(Some uvec) ~rvec:None

   308→let max_sides_per_segment = 6
   309→
   310→(* find_connect_side: given a segment's children array and a base segment number,
   311→   return the side index that connects to the base segment, or -1 if none. *)
   312→let find_connect_side ~(children : int array) ~base_seg_num =
   313→  let rec loop s =
   314→    if s >= max_sides_per_segment
   315→    then -1
   316→    else if children.(s) = base_seg_num
   317→    then s
   318→    else loop (s + 1)
   319→  in
   320→  loop 0
   321→;;
   322→
   323→(* --- find_connected_distance ----------------------------------------- *)
   324→(* BFS to find distance between two points via segment connectivity.
   325→   Packed array layout:
   326→   Header (12 ints):
   327→     [0..2]  p0.x, p0.y, p0.z
   328→     [3]     seg0
   329→     [4..6]  p1.x, p1.y, p1.z
   330→     [7]     seg1
   331→     [8]     max_depth (-1 = unlimited)
   332→     [9]     wid_flag
   333→     [10]    n_segments
   334→     [11]    check_wid_on_adjacency (0=D1 skip, 1=D2 check)
   335→
   336→   Per-segment (15 ints each):
   337→     [0..5]   children[0..5]
   338→     [6..11]  wid[0..5] — pre-evaluated WALL_IS_DOORWAY results
   339→     [12..14] center.x, center.y, center.z — pre-computed segment center
   340→
   341→   Returns: [| distance (fix) or -1; connected_segment_distance |] *)
   342→
   343→let max_loc_point_segs = 64
   344→
   345→let find_connected_distance (arr : int array) =
   346→  let p0 = arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2) in
   347→  let seg0 = arr.(3) in
   348→  let p1 = arr.(4), arr.(5), arr.(6) in
   349→  let seg1 = arr.(7) in
   350→  let max_depth_raw = arr.(8) in
   351→  let wid_flag = arr.(9) in
   352→  let n_segments = arr.(10) in
   353→  let check_wid_adj = arr.(11) in
   354→  let header = 12 in
   355→  let per_seg = 15 in
   356→  let max_depth =
   357→    if max_depth_raw > max_loc_point_segs - 2
   358→    then max_loc_point_segs - 2
   359→    else max_depth_raw
   360→  in
   361→  let seg_children s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + i) in
   362→  let seg_wid s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + 6 + i) in
   363→  let seg_center s =
   364→    let base = header + (s * per_seg) + 12 in
   365→    arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2)
   366→  in
   367→  (* Quick return: same segment *)
   368→  if seg0 = seg1
   369→  then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 0 |]
   370→  else (
   371→    (* Adjacency check *)
   372→    let conn_side = ref (-1) in
   373→    for side = 0 to 5 do
   374→      if
   375→        !conn_side = -1 && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments && seg_children seg0 side = seg1
   376→      then conn_side := side
   377→    done;
   378→    if
   379→      !conn_side <> -1
   380→      && (check_wid_adj = 0
   381→          || (seg1 >= 0 && seg1 < n_segments && seg_wid seg1 !conn_side land wid_flag <> 0)
   382→         )
   383→    then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 1 |]
   384→    else (
   385→      (* BFS *)
   386→      let visited = Array.create ~len:n_segments false in
   387→      let q_start = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   388→      let q_end = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   389→      let q_depth = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   390→      let qtail = ref 0 in
   391→      let qhead = ref 0 in
   392→      let cur_seg = ref seg0 in
   393→      let cur_depth = ref 0 in
   394→      let found = ref false in
   395→      let failed = ref false in
   396→      if seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments then visited.(seg0) <- true;
   397→      while !cur_seg <> seg1 && not !failed do
   398→        let cs = !cur_seg in
   399→        if cs < 0 || cs >= n_segments
   400→        then failed := true
   401→        else (
   402→          for sidenum = 0 to 5 do
   403→            if (not !failed) && seg_wid cs sidenum land wid_flag <> 0
   404→            then (
   405→              let child = seg_children cs sidenum in
   406→              if child >= 0 && child < n_segments && not visited.(child)
   407→              then (
   408→                let qt = !qtail in
   409→                q_start.(qt) <- cs;
   410→                q_end.(qt) <- child;
   411→                visited.(child) <- true;
   412→                q_depth.(qt) <- !cur_depth + 1;
   413→                qtail := qt + 1;
   414→                if max_depth <> -1 && !cur_depth + 1 = max_depth
   415→                then failed := true
   416→                else if max_depth = -1 && child = seg1
   417→                then (
   418→                  found := true;
   419→                  cur_seg := seg1)))
   420→          done;
   421→          if (not !found) && not !failed
   422→          then
   423→            if !qhead >= !qtail
   424→            then failed := true
   425→            else (
   426→              cur_seg := q_end.(!qhead);
   427→              cur_depth := q_depth.(!qhead);
   428→              qhead := !qhead + 1))
   429→      done;
   430→      if !failed
   431→      then [| -1; 1000 |]
   432→      else (
   433→        (* Path reconstruction: trace back from seg1 to seg0 *)
   434→        let path_centers = Array.create ~len:max_loc_point_segs (0, 0, 0) in
   435→        let num_points = ref 0 in
   436→        (* Find last queue entry ending at seg1 *)
   437→        let qt = ref (!qtail - 1) in
   438→        while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> seg1 do
   439→          qt := !qt - 1
   440→        done;
   441→        if !qt < 0
   442→        then [| -1; 1000 |]
   443→        else (
   444→          (* Walk backwards through BFS tree *)
   445→          let done_ = ref false in
   446→          while !qt >= 0 && not !done_ do
   447→            let this_seg = q_end.(!qt) in
   448→            let parent_seg = q_start.(!qt) in
   449→            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs
   450→            then (
   451→              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center this_seg;
   452→              num_points := !num_points + 1);
   453→            if parent_seg = seg0
   454→            then done_ := true
   455→            else (
   456→              qt := !qt - 1;
   457→              while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> parent_seg do
   458→                qt := !qt - 1
   459→              done;
   460→              if !qt < 0 then done_ := true)
   461→          done;
   462→          if !qt < 0 && not !done_
   463→          then [| -1; 1000 |]
   464→          else (
   465→            (* Add seg0 center *)
   466→            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments
   467→            then (
   468→              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center seg0;
   469→              num_points := !num_points + 1);
   470→            let np = !num_points in
   471→            if np = 1
   472→            then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; np |]
   473→            else (
   474→              (* Sum distances along path:
   475→                 p1 -> path_centers[1] -> ... -> path_centers[np-2] -> p0 *)
   476→              let dist = ref 0 in
   477→              dist
   478→              := Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p1 ~b:path_centers.(1)
   479→                 + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:path_centers.(np - 2);
   480→              for i = 1 to np - 3 do
   481→                dist
   482→                := !dist
   483→                   + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:path_centers.(i) ~b:path_centers.(i + 1)
   484→              done;
   485→              [| !dist; np |]))))))
   486→;;
   487→
   488→let matrix_precision = 9
   489→let matrix_max = 0x7f
   490→let relpos_precision = 10
   491→let vel_precision = 12
   492→
   493→let convert_to_byte f =
   494→  if f >= 0x00010000
   495→  then matrix_max
   496→  else if f <= -0x00010000
   497→  then -matrix_max
   498→  else f asr matrix_precision
   499→;;
   500→
   501→(* create_shortpos: compress an object's orient/pos/vel into a shortpos.
   502→   orient: 9 ints (rx,ux,fx,ry,uy,fy,rz,uz,fz)
   503→   pos: (x,y,z), vel: (x,y,z), seg_vert0: (x,y,z) position of segment vertex 0
   504→   segnum: object's segment number
   505→   Returns: (bytemat[9], xo, yo, zo, segment, velx, vely, velz) *)
   506→let create_shortpos
   507→      ~(orient : int array)
   508→      ~(pos : vec3)
   509→      ~(vel : vec3)
   510→      ~(seg_vert0 : vec3)
   511→      ~segnum
   512→  =
   513→  let bytemat = Array.init 9 ~f:(fun i -> convert_to_byte orient.(i)) in
   514→  let px, py, pz = pos in
   515→  let v0x, v0y, v0z = seg_vert0 in
   516→  let xo = (px - v0x) asr relpos_precision in
   517→  let yo = (py - v0y) asr relpos_precision in
   518→  let zo = (pz - v0z) asr relpos_precision in
   519→  let vx, vy, vz = vel in
   520→  let velx = vx asr vel_precision in
   521→  let vely = vy asr vel_precision in
   522→  let velz = vz asr vel_precision in
   523→  bytemat, xo, yo, zo, segnum, velx, vely, velz
   524→;;
   525→
   526→(* extract_shortpos: decompress a shortpos back to orient/pos/vel.
   527→   bytemat: 9 ints, xo/yo/zo: relative position shorts,
   528→   seg_vert0: (x,y,z), velx/vely/velz: velocity shorts
   529→   Returns: (orient[9], pos(x,y,z), vel(x,y,z)) *)
   530→let extract_shortpos
   531→      ~(bytemat : int array)
   532→      ~xo
   533→      ~yo
   534→      ~zo
   535→      ~(seg_vert0 : vec3)
   536→      ~velx
   537→      ~vely
   538→      ~velz
   539→  =
   540→  let orient = Array.init 9 ~f:(fun i -> bytemat.(i) lsl matrix_precision) in
   541→  let v0x, v0y, v0z = seg_vert0 in
   542→  let pos =
   543→    ( (xo lsl relpos_precision) + v0x
   544→    , (yo lsl relpos_precision) + v0y
   545→    , (zo lsl relpos_precision) + v0z )
   546→  in
   547→  let vel = velx lsl vel_precision, vely lsl vel_precision, velz lsl vel_precision in
   548→  orient, pos, vel
   549→;;
   550→
   551→(* --- create_walls_on_side --------------------------------------------- *)
   552→(* Determine side type (quad or triangulated) and compute face normals.
   553→   Combines C functions: create_walls_on_side, add_side_as_quad, add_side_as_2_triangles.
   554→   v0..v3: 4 vertex positions (as vec3), vi0..vi3: their absolute vertex indices,
   555→   has_child: whether there's a child segment on this side.
   556→   Returns (side_type, normal0, normal1). *)
   557→
   558→let side_is_quad = 1
   559→let side_is_tri_02 = 2
   560→let side_is_tri_13 = 3
   561→let plane_dist_tolerance = 250
   562→let vec_negate (x, y, z) = -x, -y, -z
   563→
   564→(* Helper: look up vertex position by absolute index from our 4 known vertices *)
   565→let make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 =
   566→  fun vi ->
   567→  if vi = vi0
   568→  then v0
   569→  else if vi = vi1
   570→  then v1
   571→  else if vi = vi2
   572→  then v2
   573→  else if vi = vi3
   574→  then v3
   575→  else failwith "create_walls_on_side: unknown vertex index"
   576→;;
   577→
   578→(* add_side_as_2_triangles logic: determine triangulation and compute normals *)
   579→let triangulate_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child =
   580→  let pos_of = make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 in
   581→  if not has_child
   582→  then (
   583→    (* Non-wall case: use dot product to choose triangulation *)
   584→    let norm = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v2 in
   585→    let vec_13 = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:v3 ~b:v1 in
   586→    let dot = Ox_math.vm_vec_dotprod ~a:norm ~b:vec_13 in
   587→    if dot >= 0
   588→    then (
   589→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v2 in
   590→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v2 ~p2:v3 in
   591→      side_is_tri_02, n0, n1)
   592→    else (
   593→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v3 in
   594→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v1 ~p1:v2 ~p2:v3 in
   595→      side_is_tri_13, n0, n1))
   596→  else (
   597→    (* Wall case: use get_verts_for_normal for consistent normals *)
   598→    let vs0, _vs1, _vs2, _vs3, _nf =
   599→      get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:vi3
   600→    in
   601→    if vs0 = vi0 || vs0 = vi2
   602→    then (
   603→      (* TRI_02 *)
   604→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf0 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:32767 in
   605→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   606→      let n0 = if nf0 <> 0 then vec_negate n0 else n0 in
   607→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf1 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi2 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   608→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   609→      let n1 = if nf1 <> 0 then vec_negate n1 else n1 in
   610→      side_is_tri_02, n0, n1)
   611→    else (
   612→      (* TRI_13 *)
   613→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf0 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   614→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   615→      let n0 = if nf0 <> 0 then vec_negate n0 else n0 in
   616→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf1 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi1 ~vb:vi2 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   617→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   618→      let n1 = if nf1 <> 0 then vec_negate n1 else n1 in
   619→      side_is_tri_13, n0, n1))
   620→;;
   621→
   622→let create_walls_on_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child =
   623→  let pos_of = make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 in
   624→  (* Step 1: Sort vertices, compute plane normal and planarity *)
   625→  let vm0, vm1, vm2, vm3, negate_flag =
   626→    get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:vi3
   627→  in
   628→  let vn = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of vm0) ~p1:(pos_of vm1) ~p2:(pos_of vm2) in
   629→  let dist =
   630→    abs (Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vm3) ~norm:vn ~planep:(pos_of vm0))
   631→  in
   632→  let vn = if negate_flag <> 0 then vec_negate vn else vn in
   633→  (* Step 2: If planar, return as quad *)
   634→  if dist <= plane_dist_tolerance
   635→  then side_is_quad, vn, vn
   636→  else (
   637→    (* Step 3: Triangulate *)
   638→    let side_type, n0, n1 =
   639→      triangulate_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child
   640→    in
   641→    (* Step 4: De-triangulation check *)
   642→    (* Build vertex list for the computed triangulation *)
   643→    let vl =
   644→      if side_type = side_is_tri_02
   645→      then [| vi0; vi1; vi2; vi2; vi3; vi0 |]
   646→      else [| vi3; vi0; vi1; vi1; vi2; vi3 |]
   647→    in
   648→    let vertnum = min vl.(0) vl.(2) in
   649→    let dist0 =
   650→      Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vl.(1)) ~norm:n1 ~planep:(pos_of vertnum)
   651→    in
   652→    let dist1 =
   653→      Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vl.(4)) ~norm:n0 ~planep:(pos_of vertnum)
   654→    in
   655→    let sign v =
   656→      if v > plane_dist_tolerance
   657→      then 1
   658→      else if v < -(plane_dist_tolerance + 1)
   659→      then -1
   660→      else 0
   661→    in
   662→    let s0 = sign dist0 in
   663→    let s1 = sign dist1 in
   664→    if s0 = 0 || s1 = 0 || s0 <> s1
   665→    then (* De-triangulate back to quad *)
   666→      side_is_quad, vn, vn
   667→    else side_type, n0, n1)
   668→;;
   669→
   670→(* --- check_norms ------------------------------------------------------ *)
   671→(* Check if two face normals are consistent (should be negations of each other).
   672→   Returns 1 if mismatch, 0 if OK. *)
   673→let check_norms ~(n0 : vec3) ~(n1 : vec3) =
   674→  let n0x, n0y, n0z = n0 in
   675→  let n1x, n1y, n1z = n1 in
   676→  if n0x <> -n1x || n0y <> -n1y || n0z <> -n1z then 1 else 0
   677→;;
   678→
   679→(* --- create_all_vertex_lists ------------------------------------------ *)
   680→(* Like create_abs_vertex_lists but returns relative vertex indices (0..7 within segment)
   681→   instead of absolute vertex indices. Takes side_type and sidenum. *)
   682→let create_all_vertex_lists ~side_type ~sidenum =
   683→  let sv = side_to_verts.(sidenum) in
   684→  match side_type with
   685→  | 1 ->
   686→    (* SIDE_IS_QUAD *)
   687→    1, [| sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(3); 0; 0 |]
   688→  | 2 ->
   689→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_02 *)
   690→    2, [| sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(2); sv.(3); sv.(0) |]
   691→  | 3 ->
   692→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_13 *)
   693→    2, [| sv.(3); sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(3) |]
   694→  | _ -> failwith (Printf.sprintf "Illegal side type %d" side_type)
   695→;;
   696→
   697→(* --- create_all_vertnum_lists ----------------------------------------- *)
   698→(* Returns vertex number indices (0..3 within side) for each face of a side.
   699→   Only depends on side_type. *)
   700→let create_all_vertnum_lists ~side_type =
   701→  match side_type with
   702→  | 1 ->
   703→    (* SIDE_IS_QUAD *)
   704→    1, [| 0; 1; 2; 3; 0; 0 |]
   705→  | 2 ->
   706→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_02 *)
   707→    2, [| 0; 1; 2; 2; 3; 0 |]
   708→  | 3 ->
   709→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_13 *)
   710→    2, [| 3; 0; 1; 1; 2; 3 |]
   711→  | _ -> failwith (Printf.sprintf "Illegal side type %d" side_type)
   712→;;
   713→
   714→(* --- trace_segs + find_point_seg ------------------------------------- *)
   715→(* Packed array layout (same 80-int-per-segment as sphere_intersects_wall):
   716→   Header (6 ints):
   717→     [0..2] p.x, p.y, p.z
   718→     [3]    segnum (hint, -1 if unknown)
   719→     [4]    n_segments
   720→     [5]    doing_lighting_hack (D1: always 0; D2: runtime flag)
   721→
   722→   Per-segment (80 ints each):
   723→     [0..5]   children[0..5]
   724→     [6..11]  side_types[0..5]
   725→     [12..19] seg_verts[0..7]
   726→     [20..55] normals: side 0..5, normals[0].xyz then normals[1].xyz
   727→     [56..79] vertex_positions: vert 0..7, xyz
   728→
   729→   Returns segment number or -1. *)
   730→
   731→let find_point_seg (arr : int array) =
   732→  let px = arr.(0) in
   733→  let py = arr.(1) in
   734→  let pz = arr.(2) in
   735→  let segnum = arr.(3) in
   736→  let n_segments = arr.(4) in
   737→  let doing_lighting_hack = arr.(5) in
   738→  let header = 6 in
   739→  let per_seg = 80 in
   740→  let get_seg_base s = header + (s * per_seg) in
   741→  let unpack_seg s =
   742→    let base = get_seg_base s in
   743→    let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + i)) in
   744→    let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + 6 + i)) in
   745→    let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + 12 + i)) in
   746→    let normals =
   747→      Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   748→        let off = base + 20 + (i * 3) in
   749→        arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   750→    in
   751→    let seg_vert_positions =
   752→      Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   753→        let off = base + 56 + (i * 3) in
   754→        arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   755→    in
   756→    children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
   757→  in
   758→  (* trace_segs: recursively find segment containing point by following
   759→     the child side with the most-negative distance. *)
   760→  let rec trace s iterations =
   761→    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments
   762→    then -1
   763→    else if iterations > 1024
   764→    then s
   765→    else (
   766→      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = unpack_seg s in
   767→      let centermask, side_dists =
   768→        get_side_dists
   769→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   770→          ~seg_verts
   771→          ~side_types
   772→          ~normals
   773→          ~seg_vert_positions
   774→      in
   775→      if centermask = 0
   776→      then s
   777→      else (
   778→        (* Make a mutable copy of side_dists so we can zero out tried sides *)
   779→        let sd = Array.copy side_dists in
   780→        let result = ref (-1) in
   781→        let continue_ = ref true in
   782→        while !continue_ do
   783→          let biggest_side = ref (-1) in
   784→          let biggest_val = ref 0 in
   785→          let bit = ref 1 in
   786→          for sn = 0 to 5 do
   787→            if centermask land !bit <> 0 && children.(sn) > -1 && sd.(sn) < !biggest_val
   788→            then (
   789→              biggest_val := sd.(sn);
   790→              biggest_side := sn);
   791→            bit := !bit lsl 1
   792→          done;
   793→          if !biggest_side <> -1
   794→          then (
   795→            sd.(!biggest_side) <- 0;
   796→            let check = trace children.(!biggest_side) (iterations + 1) in
   797→            if check <> -1
   798→            then (
   799→              result := check;
   800→              continue_ := false))
   801→          else continue_ := false
   802→        done;
   803→        !result))
   804→  in
   805→  (* find_point_seg: try trace_segs from hint, fall back to exhaustive scan *)
   806→  if segnum >= 0 && segnum < n_segments
   807→  then (
   808→    let newseg = trace segnum 0 in
   809→    if newseg <> -1
   810→    then newseg
   811→    else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   812→    then (
   813→      (* Exhaustive scan: find first segment where centermask=0 *)
   814→      let found = ref (-1) in
   815→      let s = ref 0 in
   816→      while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   817→        let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions =
   818→          unpack_seg !s
   819→        in
   820→        let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   821→          get_seg_masks
   822→            ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   823→            ~rad:0
   824→            ~seg_verts
   825→            ~side_types
   826→            ~normals
   827→            ~seg_vert_positions
   828→        in
   829→        if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   830→        incr s
   831→      done;
   832→      !found)
   833→    else -1)
   834→  else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   835→  then (
   836→    (* segnum=-1: no hint, go straight to exhaustive *)
   837→    let found = ref (-1) in
   838→    let s = ref 0 in
   839→    while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   840→      let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = unpack_seg !s in
   841→      let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   842→        get_seg_masks
   843→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   844→          ~rad:0
   845→          ~seg_verts
   846→          ~side_types
   847→          ~normals
   848→          ~seg_vert_positions
   849→      in
   850→      if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   851→      incr s
   852→    done;
   853→    !found)
   854→  else -1
   855→;;
   856→
   857→(* find_point_seg_v2: same algorithm as find_point_seg but fetches segment
   858→   data on demand via Fetch_segment_data effects instead of a pre-packed array.
   859→   The 87-int segment data uses the same layout as FVI packed segments:
   860→     [0..5] children, [6..11] side_types, [12..19] seg_verts,
   861→     [20..55] normals (side*2, xyz each), [56..79] vert positions (8 verts, xyz each),
   862→     [80..85] wid, [86] first_object.
   863→   We only need the first 80 ints (same as the old find_point_seg format). *)
   864→let unpack_seg_from_fetch (seg_data : int array) =
   865→  let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(i)) in
   866→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
   867→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
   868→  let normals =
   869→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   870→      let off = 20 + (i * 3) in
   871→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   872→  in
   873→  let seg_vert_positions =
   874→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   875→      let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   876→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   877→  in
   878→  children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
   879→;;
   880→
   881→let find_point_seg_v2 ~point:(px, py, pz) ~segnum ~n_segments ~doing_lighting_hack =
   882→  let fetch s = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data s) |> unpack_seg_from_fetch in
   883→  let rec trace s iterations =
   884→    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments
   885→    then -1
   886→    else if iterations > 1024
   887→    then s
   888→    else (
   889→      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch s in
   890→      let centermask, side_dists =
   891→        get_side_dists
   892→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   893→          ~seg_verts
   894→          ~side_types
   895→          ~normals
   896→          ~seg_vert_positions
   897→      in
   898→      if centermask = 0
   899→      then s
   900→      else (
   901→        let sd = Array.copy side_dists in
   902→        let result = ref (-1) in
   903→        let continue_ = ref true in
   904→        while !continue_ do
   905→          let biggest_side = ref (-1) in
   906→          let biggest_val = ref 0 in
   907→          let bit = ref 1 in
   908→          for sn = 0 to 5 do
   909→            if centermask land !bit <> 0 && children.(sn) > -1 && sd.(sn) < !biggest_val
   910→            then (
   911→              biggest_val := sd.(sn);
   912→              biggest_side := sn);
   913→            bit := !bit lsl 1
   914→          done;
   915→          if !biggest_side <> -1
   916→          then (
   917→            sd.(!biggest_side) <- 0;
   918→            let check = trace children.(!biggest_side) (iterations + 1) in
   919→            if check <> -1
   920→            then (
   921→              result := check;
   922→              continue_ := false))
   923→          else continue_ := false
   924→        done;
   925→        !result))
   926→  in
   927→  let exhaustive_scan () =
   928→    let found = ref (-1) in
   929→    let s = ref 0 in
   930→    while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   931→      let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch !s in
   932→      let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   933→        get_seg_masks
   934→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   935→          ~rad:0
   936→          ~seg_verts
   937→          ~side_types
   938→          ~normals
   939→          ~seg_vert_positions
   940→      in
   941→      if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   942→      incr s
   943→    done;
   944→    !found

   947→  then (
   948→    let newseg = trace segnum 0 in
   949→    if newseg <> -1
   950→    then newseg
   951→    else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   952→    then exhaustive_scan ()
   953→    else -1)
   954→  else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   955→  then exhaustive_scan ()
   956→  else -1
   957→;;
   958→
   959→let max_vertices_per_segment = 8
   960→
   961→(* pick_random_point_in_seg: pick a random point inside a segment.
   962→   Computes the segment center, picks a random vertex, then moves
   963→   a random fraction toward that vertex.
   964→   C original: gameseg.cpp pick_random_point_in_seg *)
   965→let pick_random_point_in_seg ~segnum =
   966→  let seg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data segnum) in
   967→  (* Extract 8 vertex positions from segment data *)
   968→  let verts =
   969→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   970→      let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   971→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   972→  in
   973→  let center =
   974→    compute_segment_center
   975→      ~v0:verts.(0) ~v1:verts.(1) ~v2:verts.(2) ~v3:verts.(3)
   976→      ~v4:verts.(4) ~v5:verts.(5) ~v6:verts.(6) ~v7:verts.(7)
   977→  in
   978→  let vnum = (Ox_misc.p_rand () * max_vertices_per_segment) asr 15 in
   979→  let vec2 = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:verts.(vnum) ~b:center in
   980→  let vec2 = Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:vec2 ~k:(Ox_misc.p_rand ()) in
   981→  Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:center ~b:vec2

   984→(* --- check_segment_connections ---------------------------------------- *)
   985→(* Heavy-duty error checking: validate that segment connections are consistent.
   986→   For each segment side with a child, checks that the child connects back,
   987→   vertex lists match (with correct orientation), and face normals are negations.
   988→   Returns 1 if errors detected, 0 if OK.
   989→   C original: gameseg.cpp check_segment_connections (debug-only) *)
   990→
   991→(* Effect: set a segment side's type (used for debug repair of mismatched triangulation) *)
   992→type _ Effect.t += Set_segment_side_type : (int * int * int) -> unit Effect.t
   993→
   994→let check_segment_connections ~highest_segment_index =
   995→  let errors = ref 0 in
   996→  for segnum = 0 to highest_segment_index do
   997→    let seg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data segnum) in
   998→    let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(i)) in
   999→    let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
  1000→    let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
  1001→    for sidenum = 0 to 5 do
  1002→      let num_faces, vertex_list =
  1003→        create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type:side_types.(sidenum) ~seg_verts ~sidenum
  1004→      in
  1005→      let csegnum = children.(sidenum) in
  1006→      if csegnum >= 0
  1007→      then (
  1008→        let cseg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data csegnum) in
  1009→        let cseg_children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> cseg_data.(i)) in
  1010→        let cseg_side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> cseg_data.(6 + i)) in
  1011→        let cseg_seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> cseg_data.(12 + i)) in
  1012→        let csidenum =
  1013→          find_connect_side ~children:cseg_children ~base_seg_num:segnum
  1014→        in
  1015→        if csidenum = -1
  1016→        then errors := 1
  1017→        else (
  1018→          let con_num_faces, con_vertex_list =
  1019→            create_abs_vertex_lists
  1020→              ~side_type:cseg_side_types.(csidenum)
  1021→              ~seg_verts:cseg_seg_verts
  1022→              ~sidenum:csidenum
  1023→          in
  1024→          if con_num_faces <> num_faces
  1025→          then errors := 1
  1026→          else if num_faces = 1
  1027→          then (
  1028→            (* Find rotation offset t where con_vertex_list[t] = vertex_list[0] *)
  1029→            let t = ref 0 in
  1030→            while !t < 4 && con_vertex_list.(!t) <> vertex_list.(0) do
  1031→              incr t
  1032→            done;
  1033→            if !t = 4
  1034→               || vertex_list.(0) <> con_vertex_list.(!t)
  1035→               || vertex_list.(1) <> con_vertex_list.((!t + 3) mod 4)
  1036→               || vertex_list.(2) <> con_vertex_list.((!t + 2) mod 4)
  1037→               || vertex_list.(3) <> con_vertex_list.((!t + 1) mod 4)
  1038→            then errors := 1
  1039→            else (
  1040→              (* check_norms(segnum, sidenum, 0, csegnum, csidenum, 0) *)
  1041→              let off0 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1042→              let n0 = seg_data.(off0), seg_data.(off0 + 1), seg_data.(off0 + 2) in
  1043→              let coff0 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1044→              let n1 = cseg_data.(coff0), cseg_data.(coff0 + 1), cseg_data.(coff0 + 2) in
  1045→              errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0 ~n1))
  1046→          else (
  1047→            (* num_faces = 2: triangulated side *)
  1048→            if vertex_list.(1) = con_vertex_list.(1)
  1049→            then (
  1050→              if vertex_list.(4) <> con_vertex_list.(4)
  1051→                 || vertex_list.(0) <> con_vertex_list.(2)
  1052→                 || vertex_list.(2) <> con_vertex_list.(0)
  1053→                 || vertex_list.(3) <> con_vertex_list.(5)
  1054→                 || vertex_list.(5) <> con_vertex_list.(3)
  1055→              then (
  1056→                (* Flip the connected side's type: type = 5 - type *)
  1057→                let new_type = 5 - cseg_side_types.(csidenum) in
  1058→                Effect.perform (Set_segment_side_type (csegnum, csidenum, new_type)))
  1059→              else (
  1060→                (* check_norms face 0 vs 0, face 1 vs 1 *)
  1061→                let off0 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1062→                let n0_0 =
  1063→                  seg_data.(off0), seg_data.(off0 + 1), seg_data.(off0 + 2)
  1064→                in
  1065→                let coff0 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1066→                let n1_0 =
  1067→                  cseg_data.(coff0), cseg_data.(coff0 + 1), cseg_data.(coff0 + 2)
  1068→                in
  1069→                errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0:n0_0 ~n1:n1_0;
  1070→                let off1 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 1) * 3 in
  1071→                let n0_1 =
  1072→                  seg_data.(off1), seg_data.(off1 + 1), seg_data.(off1 + 2)
  1073→                in
  1074→                let coff1 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 1) * 3 in
  1075→                let n1_1 =
  1076→                  cseg_data.(coff1), cseg_data.(coff1 + 1), cseg_data.(coff1 + 2)
  1077→                in
  1078→                errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0:n0_1 ~n1:n1_1))
  1079→            else if vertex_list.(1) <> con_vertex_list.(4)
  1080→                    || vertex_list.(4) <> con_vertex_list.(1)
  1081→                    || vertex_list.(0) <> con_vertex_list.(5)
  1082→                    || vertex_list.(5) <> con_vertex_list.(0)

  1090→              (* check_norms face 0 vs 1, face 1 vs 0 *)
  1091→              let off0 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1092→              let n0_0 =
  1093→                seg_data.(off0), seg_data.(off0 + 1), seg_data.(off0 + 2)
  1094→              in
  1095→              let coff1 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 1) * 3 in
  1096→              let n1_1 =
  1097→                cseg_data.(coff1), cseg_data.(coff1 + 1), cseg_data.(coff1 + 2)
  1098→              in
  1099→              errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0:n0_0 ~n1:n1_1;
  1100→              let off1 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 1) * 3 in
  1101→              let n0_1 =
  1102→                seg_data.(off1), seg_data.(off1 + 1), seg_data.(off1 + 2)
  1103→              in
  1104→              let coff0 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1105→              let n1_0 =
  1106→                cseg_data.(coff0), cseg_data.(coff0 + 1), cseg_data.(coff0 + 2)
  1107→              in
  1108→              errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0:n0_1 ~n1:n1_0))))

## Summary

I've found the C implementations of both functions and verified the existing OCaml code. Here's what you need to know:

### C Implementation: `find_connected_distance_segments`

**Location:** `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/main_d2/gameseg.cpp:2572-2580`

```cpp
fix find_connected_distance_segments( int seg0, int seg1, int depth, int wid_flag)
{
    vms_vector    p0, p1;

    compute_segment_center(&p0, &Segments[seg0]);
    compute_segment_center(&p1, &Segments[seg1]);

    return find_connected_distance(&p0, seg0, &p1, seg1, depth, wid_flag);
}
```

**Function:** This is a thin wrapper that computes segment centers and delegates to `find_connected_distance`.

---

### C Implementation: `find_connected_distance`

**Location:** `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/main_d2/gameseg.cpp:1256-1475`

This function has two paths:

**When `USE_OX_BRIDGE` is defined (lines 1258-1324):**
- Performs quick returns (same segment or adjacent segments)
- Checks/flushes a distance cache
- Packs all segment data into a contiguous array (header + 15 ints per segment)
- Calls `cd_ox_find_connected_distance` (OCaml bridge)
- Caches results

**When `USE_OX_BRIDGE` is NOT defined (lines 1326-1475):**
- Implements a full BFS algorithm:
  - Validates inputs, handles cache checks
  - Builds a queue of segments to explore, tracking visited status and depth
  - Checks doorway flags (`WALL_IS_DOORWAY`) for each side
  - Stops when target segment is found or max depth/queue exhaustion
  - Reconstructs the path through segment centers and sums distances along it
  - Returns final distance or -1 if unreachable

**Key variables:**
- `Connected_segment_distance` (global): stores the "connected segment distance" (number of segments along the path or 1000 if unreachable)
- Cache: 8-entry FIFO with per-frame flushing

**D1 vs D2 difference:**
- D1 (line 1152 in main_d1/gameseg.cpp): `check_wid_on_adjacency = 0` (skip WID check for direct adjacency)
- D2 (line 1304 in main_d2/gameseg.cpp): `check_wid_on_adjacency = 1` (enforce WID check even for adjacent segments)

---

### Existing OCaml Code

**Location:** `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/ox_gameseg.ml:323-486`

The OCaml implementation is **already complete** with a fully ported BFS algorithm:

```ocaml
let find_connected_distance (arr : int array) =
  (* Unpacks 12-int header + per-segment 15-int data *)
  (* Implements full BFS: quick returns, adjacency check, queue-based search *)
  (* Reconstructs path and sums distances *)
  (* Returns [| distance (fix) or -1; connected_segment_distance |] *)
```

The OCaml version:
- Accepts the same packed array format as the C bridge callsite
- Handles D1 vs D2 via `check_wid_adj` flag (header[11])
- Implements identical BFS logic
- Returns a 2-element array: `[distance, connected_segment_distance]`
- Already being called via `cd_ox_find_connected_distance` external in the bridge

**Related gameseg functions already in OCaml:**
- `find_point_seg` (lines 731-855): locate a point in segment space
- `find_point_seg_v2` (lines 881-957): on-demand segment data fetch version
- `pick_random_point_in_seg` (lines 965-982): random point in segment
- `check_segment_connections` (lines 994-1112): validate segment topology
- `compute_segment_center` (lines 41-73): 8-vertex averaging
- `create_shortpos` / `extract_shortpos` (lines 506-549): compact object encoding

---

### Bridge Declaration

**Location:** `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/main_d2/gameseg.h:93`

```cpp
extern fix find_connected_distance(vms_vector *p0, int seg0, vms_vector *p1, int seg1, int max_depth, int wid_flag);
```

And there's also (implicitly from the code) `find_connected_distance_segments` which is just a wrapper.

---

### Summary

- **`find_connected_distance_segments`**: Simple wrapper that extracts segment centers and calls `find_connected_distance`
- **`find_connected_distance`**: Full BFS implementation to find reachable-distance between two segments; already fully ported to OCaml
- **OCaml version is production-ready**: The `ox_gameseg.ml:find_connected_distance` function is complete and matches the C algorithm exactly, including the D1/D2 difference

content.thinking:
This is a very thin wrapper. It just computes two segment centers and calls `find_connected_distance`. Both `compute_segment_center` and `find_connected_distance` are already ported. 

The function is called from callers like `do_thief_frame`, `escort_create_path_to_goal`, `move_object_to_goal`. Since it's a thin wrapper, I need to:

1. Add it to OCaml (ox_gameseg.ml)
2. Wire up a bridge call
3. Add USE_OX_BRIDGE path in the C++ code
4. Update gen_callgraph.py


5. Now I'm checking how `find_connected_distance` is already bridged from OCaml to understand the pattern I should follow.

(* --- find_connected_distance ----------------------------------------- *)
(* BFS to find distance between two points via segment connectivity.
   Packed array layout:
   Header (12 ints):
     [0..2]  p0.x, p0.y, p0.z
     [3]     seg0
     [4..6]  p1.x, p1.y, p1.z
     [7]     seg1
     [8]     max_depth (-1 = unlimited)
     [9]     wid_flag
     [10]    n_segments
     [11]    check_wid_on_adjacency (0=D1 skip, 1=D2 check)

   Per-segment (15 ints each):
     [0..5]   children[0..5]
     [6..11]  wid[0..5] — pre-evaluated WALL_IS_DOORWAY results
     [12..14] center.x, center.y, center.z — pre-computed segment center

   Returns: [| distance (fix) or -1; connected_segment_distance |] *)

let max_loc_point_segs = 64

let find_connected_distance (arr : int array) =
  let p0 = arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2) in
  let seg0 = arr.(3) in
  let p1 = arr.(4), arr.(5), arr.(6) in
  let seg1 = arr.(7) in
  let max_depth_raw = arr.(8) in
  let wid_flag = arr.(9) in
  let n_segments = arr.(10) in

345→let find_connected_distance (arr : int array) =
   346→  let p0 = arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2) in
   347→  let seg0 = arr.(3) in
   348→  let p1 = arr.(4), arr.(5), arr.(6) in
   349→  let seg1 = arr.(7) in
   350→  let max_depth_raw = arr.(8) in
   351→  let wid_flag = arr.(9) in
   352→  let n_segments = arr.(10) in
   353→  let check_wid_adj = arr.(11) in
   354→  let header = 12 in
   355→  let per_seg = 15 in
   356→  let max_depth =
   357→    if max_depth_raw > max_loc_point_segs - 2
   358→    then max_loc_point_segs - 2
   359→    else max_depth_raw
   360→  in
   361→  let seg_children s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + i) in
   362→  let seg_wid s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + 6 + i) in
   363→  let seg_center s =
   364→    let base = header + (s * per_seg) + 12 in
   365→    arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2)
   366→  in
   367→  (* Quick return: same segment *)
   368→  if seg0 = seg1
   369→  then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 0 |]
   370→  else (
   371→    (* Adjacency check *)
   372→    let conn_side = ref (-1) in
   373→    for side = 0 to 5 do
   374→      if
   375→        !conn_side = -1 && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments && seg_children seg0 side = seg1
   376→      then conn_side := side
   377→    done;
   378→    if
   379→      !conn_side <> -1
   380→      && (check_wid_adj = 0
   381→          || (seg1 >= 0 && seg1 < n_segments && seg_wid seg1 !conn_side land wid_flag <> 0)
   382→         )
   383→    then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 1 |]
   384→    else (
   385→      (* BFS *)
   386→      let visited = Array.create ~len:n_segments false in
   387→      let q_start = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   388→      let q_end = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   389→      let q_depth = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   390→      let qtail = ref 0 in
   391→      let qhead = ref 0 in
   392→      let cur_seg = ref seg0 in
   393→      let cur_depth = ref 0 in
   394→      let found = ref false in
   395→      let failed = ref false in
   396→      if seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments then visited.(seg0) <- true;
   397→      while !cur_seg <> seg1 && not !failed do
   398→        let cs = !cur_seg in
   399→        if cs < 0 || cs >= n_segments
   400→        then failed := true
   401→        else (
   402→          for sidenum = 0 to 5 do
   403→            if (not !failed) && seg_wid cs sidenum land wid_flag <> 0
   404→            then (
   405→              let child = seg_children cs sidenum in
   406→              if child >= 0 && child < n_segments && not visited.(child)
   407→              then (
   408→                let qt = !qtail in
   409→                q_start.(qt) <- cs;
   410→                q_end.(qt) <- child;
   411→                visited.(child) <- true;
   412→                q_depth.(qt) <- !cur_depth + 1;
   413→                qtail := qt + 1;
   414→                if max_depth <> -1 && !cur_depth + 1 = max_depth
   415→                then failed := true
   416→                else if max_depth = -1 && child = seg1
   417→                then (
   418→                  found := true;
   419→                  cur_seg := seg1)))
   420→          done;
   421→          if (not !found) && not !failed
   422→          then
   423→            if !qhead >= !qtail
   424→            then failed := true
   425→            else (
   426→              cur_seg := q_end.(!qhead);
   427→              cur_depth := q_depth.(!qhead);
   428→              qhead := !qhead + 1))
   429→      done;
   430→      if !failed
   431→      then [| -1; 1000 |]
   432→      else (
   433→        (* Path reconstruction: trace back from seg1 to seg0 *)
   434→        let path_centers = Array.create ~len:max_loc_point_segs (0, 0, 0) in
   435→        let num_points = ref 0 in
   436→        (* Find last queue entry ending at seg1 *)
   437→        let qt = ref (!qtail - 1) in
   438→        while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> seg1 do
   439→          qt := !qt - 1
   440→        done;
   441→        if !qt < 0
   442→        then [| -1; 1000 |]
   443→        else (
   444→          (* Walk backwards through BFS tree *)
   445→          let done_ = ref false in
   446→          while !qt >= 0 && not !done_ do
   447→            let this_seg = q_end.(!qt) in
   448→            let parent_seg = q_start.(!qt) in
   449→            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs
   450→            then (
   451→              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center this_seg;
   452→              num_points := !num_points + 1);
   453→            if parent_seg = seg0
   454→            then done_ := true
   455→            else (
   456→              qt := !qt - 1;
   457→              while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> parent_seg do
   458→                qt := !qt - 1
   459→              done;
   460→              if !qt < 0 then done_ := true)
   461→          done;
   462→          if !qt < 0 && not !done_
   463→          then [| -1; 1000 |]
   464→          else (
   465→            (* Add seg0 center *)
   466→            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments
   467→            then (
   468→              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center seg0;
   469→              num_points := !num_points + 1);
   470→            let np = !num_points in
   471→            if np = 1
   472→            then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; np |]
   473→            else (
   474→              (* Sum distances along path:
   475→                 p1 -> path_centers[1] -> ... -> path_centers[np-2] -> p0 *)
   476→              let dist = ref 0 in
   477→              dist
   478→              := Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p1 ~b:path_centers.(1)
   479→                 + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:path_centers.(np - 2);
   480→              for i = 1 to np - 3 do
   481→                dist
   482→                := !dist
   483→                   + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:path_centers.(i) ~b:path_centers.(i + 1)
   484→              done;
   485→              [| !dist; np |]))))))
   486→;;
   487→
   488→let matrix_precision = 9
   489→let matrix_max = 0x7f
   490→let relpos_precision = 10
   491→let vel_precision = 12
   492→
   493→let convert_to_byte f =
   494→  if f >= 0x00010000

let find_connected_distance (arr : int array) =
  let p0 = arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2) in
  let seg0 = arr.(3) in
  let p1 = arr.(4), arr.(5), arr.(6) in
  let seg1 = arr.(7) in
  let max_depth_raw = arr.(8) in
  let wid_flag = arr.(9) in
  let n_segments = arr.(10) in
  let check_wid_adj = arr.(11) in
  let header = 12 in
  let per_seg = 15 in
  let max_depth =
    if max_depth_raw > max_loc_point_segs - 2
    then max_loc_point_segs - 2
    else max_depth_raw
  in
  let seg_children s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + i) in
  let seg_wid s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + 6 + i) in
  let seg_center s =
    let base = header + (s * per_seg) + 12 in
    arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2)
  in
  (* Quick return: same segment *)
  if seg0 = seg1
  then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 0 |]
  else (
    (* Adjacency check *)
    let conn_side = ref (-1) in
    for side = 0 to 5 do
      if
        !conn_side = -1 && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments && seg_children seg0 side = seg1
      then conn_side := side
    done;
    if
      !conn_side <> -1
      && (check_wid_adj = 0
          || (seg1 >= 0 && seg1 < n_segments && seg_wid seg1 !conn_side land wid_flag <> 0)
         )
    then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 1 |]
    else (
      (* BFS *)
      let visited = Array.create ~len:n_segments false in
      let q_start = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
      let q_end = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
      let q_depth = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
      let qtail = ref 0 in
      let qhead = ref 0 in
      let cur_seg = ref seg0 in
      let cur_depth = ref 0 in
      let found = ref false in
      let failed = ref false in
      if seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments then visited.(seg0) <- true;
      while !cur_seg <> seg1 && not !failed do
        let cs = !cur_seg in
        if cs < 0 || cs >= n_segments
        then failed := true
        else (
          for sidenum = 0 to 5 do
            if (not !failed) && seg_wid cs sidenum land wid_flag <> 0
            then (
              let child = seg_children cs sidenum in
              if child >= 0 && child < n_segments && not visited.(child)
              then (
                let qt = !qtail in
                q_start.(qt) <- cs;
                q_end.(qt) <- child;
                visited.(child) <- true;
                q_depth.(qt) <- !cur_depth + 1;
                qtail := qt + 1;
                if max_depth <> -1 && !cur_depth + 1 = max_depth
                then failed := true
                else if max_depth = -1 && child = seg1
                then (
                  found := true;
                  cur_seg := seg1)))
          done;
          if (not !found) && not !failed
          then
            if !qhead >= !qtail
            then failed := true
            else (
              cur_seg := q_end.(!qhead);
              cur_depth := q_depth.(!qhead);
              qhead := !qhead + 1))
      done;
      if !failed
      then [| -1; 1000 |]
      else (
        (* Path reconstruction: trace back from seg1 to seg0 *)
        let path_centers = Array.create ~len:max_loc_point_segs (0, 0, 0) in
        let num_points = ref 0 in
        (* Find last queue entry ending at seg1 *)
        let qt = ref (!qtail - 1) in
        while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> seg1 do
          qt := !qt - 1
        done;
        if !qt < 0
        then [| -1; 1000 |]
        else (
          (* Walk backwards through BFS tree *)
          let done_ = ref false in
          while !qt >= 0 && not !done_ do
            let this_seg = q_end.(!qt) in
            let parent_seg = q_start.(!qt) in
            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs
            then (
              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center this_seg;
              num_points := !num_points + 1);
            if parent_seg = seg0
            then done_ := true
            else (
              qt := !qt - 1;
              while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> parent_seg do
                qt := !qt - 1
              done;
              if !qt < 0 then done_ := true)
          done;
          if !qt < 0 && not !done_
          then [| -1; 1000 |]
          else (
            (* Add seg0 center *)
            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments
            then (
              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center seg0;
              num_points := !num_points + 1);
            let np = !num_points in
            if np = 1
            then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; np |]
            else (
              (* Sum distances along path:
                 p1 -> path_centers[1] -> ... -> path_centers[np-2] -> p0 *)
              let dist = ref 0 in
              dist
              := Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p1 ~b:path_centers.(1)
                 + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:path_centers.(np - 2);
              for i = 1 to np - 3 do
                dist
                := !dist
                   + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:path_centers.(i) ~b:path_centers.(i + 1)
              done;
              [| !dist; np |]))))))
;;

(* --- find_connected_distance_segments --------------------------------- *)
(* Thin wrapper: compute segment centers from per-segment data,
   then delegate to find_connected_distance.
   Packed array layout:
   Header (6 ints):
     [0] seg0  [1] seg1  [2] depth  [3] wid_flag
     [4] n_segments  [5] check_wid_on_adjacency
   Per-segment (15 ints each, same as find_connected_distance):
     [0..5]   children[0..5]
     [6..11]  wid[0..5]
     [12..14] center.x, center.y, center.z
   Returns: [| distance (fix) or -1; connected_segment_distance |] *)
let find_connected_distance_segments (arr : int array) =
  let seg0 = arr.(0) in
  let seg1 = arr.(1) in
  let depth = arr.(2) in
  let wid_flag = arr.(3) in
  let n_segs = arr.(4) in
  let check_wid_adj = arr.(5) in
  let hdr = 6 in
  let per_seg = 15 in
  (* Extract pre-computed centers for seg0 and seg1 *)
  let base0 = hdr + seg0 * per_seg + 12 in
  let p0x = arr.(base0) in
  let p0y = arr.(base0 + 1) in
  let p0z = arr.(base0 + 2) in
  let base1 = hdr + seg1 * per_seg + 12 in
  let p1x = arr.(base1) in
  let p1y = arr.(base1 + 1) in
  let p1z = arr.(base1 + 2) in
  (* Build find_connected_distance packed array: 12-int header + per-seg data *)
  let fcd_len = 12 + n_segs * per_seg in
  let fcd = Array.create ~len:fcd_len 0 in
  fcd.(0) <- p0x; fcd.(1) <- p0y; fcd.(2) <- p0z;
  fcd.(3) <- seg0;
  fcd.(4) <- p1x; fcd.(5) <- p1y; fcd.(6) <- p1z;
  fcd.(7) <- seg1;
  fcd.(8) <- depth;
  fcd.(9) <- wid_flag;
  fcd.(10) <- n_segs;
  fcd.(11) <- check_wid_adj;
  Array.blit ~src:arr ~src_pos:hdr ~dst:fcd ~dst_pos:12 ~len:(n_segs * per_seg);
  find_connected_distance fcd
;;
